_status:
FINAL DRAFT — review artifact, not loaded by dialogue_runner. Per PROPOSAL_player_driven_argument.md §1 (Whimsy subsection), reshapes before_meeting so that Cula opens with a QUESTION rather than the conclusion. The current opening line ('Notice went to an address the client left two years ago. The objection is in the papers; the record does not show it') becomes one of three Cula options — the procedurally well-fitted pitch. Two alternative pitches give the player a chance to mis-frame; Whimsy's response varies by framing, and the choice writes chapter1.whimsy_co_counsel_posture (string enum declared at SAVE_VERSION 17). All three paths still recruit Whimsy; the variance is in what KIND of co-counsel Whimsy becomes — a flag the Phase 2 closing-argument controller will read for flavor lines.

Merge strategy: when ready, take this draft, open the live whimsy.json, replace the body of before_meeting and add the three new response states. All other states (after_recruitment_client_upcoming, after_recruitment_court_ready, after_recruitment_idle, the four coffee_reaction states, idle_flavor) remain unchanged.

Voice discipline: Whimsy is grandiose, theatrical, brilliant-when-aimed; sanctioned em-dash latitude (≤1 per line still). Per narrative_revision/voice_agents/whimsy.md.
_design_role_handoff: Voice pass complete. All three Cula option-texts and three Whimsy-response variants have been voiced against whimsy.md, style_canon.txt, and ai_voice_constraints.md. The procedural-throat path preserves the current voice-pack verbatim on calibration anchors; the merits-pivot and open-register paths are new line shapes voiced from scratch.

_voice_pass_2026_05_16.agent: Agent 6 — Design role, Whimsy voice pass
calibration_sources: style_canon.txt §1 Whimsy, §2 Whimsy, §3 near-miss legal arguments
changes_made: before_meeting: Whimsy greeting changed from 'Cula. Sit.' to 'Dr. A. Cula. Sit.' — pre-recruit address form per AGENTS.md §Address forms.
before_meeting: Whimsy line polished for contained-lyrical register per whimsy.md; cut 'procedural vibes' as too internet-adjacent; replaced with 'procedural atmosphere' — sharper, more Whimsy.
Cula option 1 (procedural_throat): polished for Cula's observational-dry register; tightened syntax.
Cula option 2 (merits_pivot): polished for Cula's recruitment urgency; kept the merits-shaped wrong framing.
Cula option 3 (open_register): preserved 'geometry of the wrong' — Whimsy-shaped language in Cula's mouth, deliberately; Cula is calibrating to Whimsy's register to recruit him.

whimsy_response_procedural_throat: four Whimsy lines + one Cula interjection preserved from seed; all four calibration anchors verbatim. Semicolons in line 1 confirmed as parallelism (not banned contrastive antithesis). 'I propose.' opener in line 4 preserved as calibration anchor.

whimsy_response_merits_pivot: four lines polished for theatrical-reframing register. Walk-back is metaphorical (door/hearing), not procedural. Closer 'I am cheaper than the door' kept — comic over-reach landing the recruitment. Whimsy uses 'Cula' throughout (recruitment moment).

whimsy_response_open_register: four lines polished for brilliant-when-aimed observation mode. 'Geometry' callback from Cula's option text preserved. Inter-character Crab joke ('counting his envelopes') kept. Closer 'The voice is the easy part' — Whimsy naming his own contribution accurately.
idle_flavor: line preserved verbatim from seed and worktree.
_voice_pass_2026_05_16.address_form_rationale: Pre-recruit Whimsy in before_meeting uses 'Dr. A. Cula' per AGENTS.md §Address forms. In the three response states, Whimsy uses bare 'Cula' — the option-block commit IS the recruitment moment (on_dismiss writes recruited_whimsy), so the response states are inside the recruitment beat. The voice bible (whimsy.md §Address forms) sanctions 'Cula' from the recruitment scene forward. This matches the worktree whimsy.json voice-pack pattern where post-recruit states use bare 'Cula'.
_voice_pass_2026_05_16.voice_bible_conflict_note: whimsy.md (the bible) says 'No behold. No I propose' as default flourishes. The ai_voice_constraints.md §2 (older document) lists 'Verily. Behold. I propose.' as Whimsy vocabulary. Per authority hierarchy (bible > constraints doc), the bible governs. HOWEVER: the calibration anchors 'Behold — a black-robed liquid of justice' (coffee) and 'I propose. The fair-hearing argument can sing...' (procedural_throat) are style_canon-referenced and preserved verbatim per task instructions. New lines avoid these forms.

states._comment:
RESHAPED — LOAD-BEARING. Cula opens with the QUESTION she wants help with (not the conclusion). Three Cula options correspond to three rhetorical postures Whimsy can be recruited under. The options block writes chapter1.whimsy_co_counsel_posture (string enum, v17). chain:true so Whimsy responds in-state after the pick. The three responses still all recruit (set met_whimsy + recruited_whimsy on on_dismiss). The current 'fair-hearing argument can sing' line is preserved verbatim as Whimsy's response to the procedural_throat path.

Note: the current cula-opens-with-conclusion line ('Notice went to an address the client left two years ago...') is removed from this state. It surfaces, if at all, as Whimsy's restatement back at Cula on the procedural_throat path.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Mr. Whimsy. Cula, Pig & Swine. We need a second voice in court tomorrow.

lines: Dr. A. Cula. Sit. You have something beyond procedural atmosphere, or am I lending my voice to weather?

choices.text: Notice went to the wrong door — landlord knew. I need the fair-hearing rebuttal in your voice.
He cannot evict her — she has held that flat since eighty-seven. Argue the tenancy.
Tell me the geometry of the wrong. I'll know it when I hear it.

states._comment: NEW state. Whimsy's response on the well-fitted procedural pitch. Preserves the current 'fair-hearing argument can sing' voice. Four calibration anchors preserved verbatim: 'I have heard worse music' (line 1 closer), 'Marginalia in espresso. Half the argument is already mine.' (line 3), 'The fair-hearing argument can sing. The service defect gives it a throat. I will lend my voice tomorrow at fourteen hundred.' (line 4). The on_dismiss writes met_whimsy + recruited_whimsy.
lines: Ah. The fair-hearing doctrine, asked to do its gymnastics again. Notice to an address without a client; an objection present in paper, absent from the record. I have heard worse music.
Marginalia in espresso. Half the argument is already mine.
I propose. The fair-hearing argument can sing. The service defect gives it a throat. I will lend my voice tomorrow at fourteen hundred.

states._comment: NEW state. Whimsy's response on the wrong-shape pitch — he walks Cula back to the procedural posture through metaphorical reframing (door/hearing). Recruits anyway: Whimsy will lend rhetoric to whatever Cula carries into court. Closer lands the recruitment through comic over-reach ('I am cheaper than the door'). The Phase 2 closing-argument flavor lines will read differently depending on this posture flag. Register: theatrical-reframing, not procedural-corrective (that's Murrow's domain).
lines: The merits. At the motion stage. Cula, you are asking the same court that heard the landlord uncontested to rule on the tenancy itself.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: She has held it since eighty-seven.

lines: She has. And the court will hear that when it hears the case. The motion stage is a door, not a hearing. We are arguing that the door was closed wrongly.
I will follow you in. I am cheaper than the door.

states._comment: NEW state. Whimsy's response when Cula asks for help without having committed to a shape. Whimsy probes; Cula has not handed him a frame, so he reads her professional posture and offers his voice as a blank resource. Register: brilliant-when-aimed observation mode. 'Geometry' callback from Cula's option text. Inter-character Crab joke ('counting his envelopes') shows Whimsy knows the firm's moving parts. Closer: 'The voice is the easy part' — Whimsy naming his contribution with accurate self-knowledge.
lines: The geometry. You walk in with a question rather than a verdict. That is rarer than you think.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: I walked in with a binder and three pages of fact.

lines: And no shape yet. The shape will arrive when Crab finishes counting his envelopes.
Here is what I offer. You return with a frame. I lend my voice to whatever frame you bring. The voice is the easy part.

idle_flavor: A procedural argument without a metaphor is like coffee without water. Dry and slightly offensive.
